evals.name: generate-full-generic-saas
evals.prompt: Generate the full documentation for this project into a `docs/` folder at the repository root. The project is an internal time-tracking and billing app called TeamBilling. Use the following sources: `project-state.md`, everything inside `features/`, all migrations in `db/migrations/`, `CONVENTIONS.md`, `DECISIONS.md`. The user roles are: admin, manager, employee, customer. The `hourly_rate` field is strictly confidential — visible only to admins and to the customer it concerns, hidden from managers and employees. The documentation must be readable by a non-technical manager who has never opened a code editor.
evals.expected_output: Complete `docs/` tree: README.md, OVERVIEW.md, FEATURES.md, reference/ (schema, roles, conventions, decisions/), how-to/ per feature, explanation/ (architecture, agents), onboarding/README-dev.md. A `project-config.md` is created under `.claude/skills/doc-generator/`. Every file carries a `Last updated` timestamp. No undefined jargon in README/OVERVIEW/FEATURES/how-to files.

assertions.text: docs/README.md exists and contains today's date
docs/OVERVIEW.md exists and is under 150 lines
docs/FEATURES.md exists and lists every feature present in project-state.md
docs/reference/schema.md exists and lists at least 3 tables derived from the migrations
docs/reference/roles.md exists and lists all 4 roles (admin, manager, employee, customer)
docs/how-to/ contains at least one -guide.md file per finished feature
docs/onboarding/README-dev.md exists
No file in docs/ exceeds 150 lines
README.md does not contain acronyms like 'ORM', 'RLS', 'RPC' without a parenthetical definition
README.md, OVERVIEW.md, FEATURES.md do not use first-person wording ('we', 'I have', 'our team')
A file at .claude/skills/doc-generator/project-config.md is created
The hourly_rate confidentiality rule is documented in reference/roles.md or OVERVIEW.md

evals.name: update-after-feature-closure
evals.prompt: The feature `email-invites` has just been closed and shipped to production. Update the documentation to reflect the closure. Sources to read: features/email-invites.md, features/email-invites-spec.md, the migration db/migrations/20260601000001_email_invites.sql, and the existing documentation under docs/. Assume docs/ already contains the structure produced by a previous `generate` run (FEATURES.md with email-invites still marked in progress, no how-to/email-invites-guide.md yet). Make surgical edits: touch only what actually needs updating.
evals.expected_output: FEATURES.md: the email-invites row moves from the 'in progress' section to the 'done' section, with a closure date. A new how-to/email-invites-guide.md is created with sections 'What it does', 'Who can use it', 'How it works', 'How to use it', 'Limits', 'Technical reference'. No other files are modified, except possibly README.md for the 'features shipped' counter. Timestamps updated on every touched file.

assertions.text: docs/FEATURES.md has an email-invites entry in the 'done' table
docs/FEATURES.md no longer lists email-invites under 'in progress'
docs/how-to/email-invites-guide.md exists
docs/how-to/email-invites-guide.md contains the sections 'What it does', 'How it works', 'How to use it'
docs/how-to/email-invites-guide.md is under 150 lines
At most 3 files are modified in total (FEATURES.md + the new how-to + optionally README.md)
Touched files carry today's date
No file under docs/reference/ is modified

evals.name: schema-after-migration
evals.prompt: A new migration has been applied: db/migrations/20260601000001_email_invites.sql. Update only reference/schema.md (and reference/roles.md if access policies change) to reflect the new tables and columns. Do not touch anything else. Assume reference/schema.md already exists from a previous run.
evals.expected_output: reference/schema.md is updated: new tables or columns from the migration are added with their types, access rules, and a one-line business explanation. If the migration touches access policies, reference/roles.md is updated too. No other file is modified. The 'Latest migration' header in schema.md is bumped to the new filename.

assertions.text: docs/reference/schema.md has been modified
docs/reference/schema.md mentions the migration filename 20260601000001_email_invites.sql in its header
No file outside docs/reference/ has been modified
docs/reference/schema.md stays under 150 lines (or is cleanly split)
New schema elements are described with a plain business sentence, not raw SQL only
